intro.speaker: Varn
intro.text: You again. I remember you — or something shaped like memory of you. The dungeon gave me back, as it gives everything back. It does not release what it has already claimed.

responses.text: I need to understand what happened.
The chains still hold you here.

remember_dying.speaker: Varn
remember_dying.text: Fragments. The shape of your face. The sound of the chains breaking. And then — nothing, and then this. I do not know how many times this has already happened. The dungeon does not keep records it intends to share.

what_happened.speaker: Varn
what_happened.text: The dungeon keeps its own counsel on that. I know only that I was, then was not, and now am again. The chains that bind me are the same chains — they do not care whether I remember them. They hold regardless.

still_here.speaker: Varn
still_here.text: Still here. Still holding. The dungeon gave me back but did not return what the chains took from me. I am less than I was. You can see that, I think.

responses.text: What do you need from me?

does_it_change.speaker: Varn
does_it_change.text: No. The work is the same. The chaos still erodes the structure. Whether I remember you changes nothing about what must be done. Perhaps that is the point.

same_task.speaker: Varn
same_task.text: The same task. I would apologize for that if it would mean anything. It does not.

what_do_you_want.speaker: Varn
what_do_you_want.text: The corrupted things still walk near this post. Clear them. The structure needs it. Whatever I am now, I am still holding the line — I need you to give it something to hold against.

agree.speaker: Varn
agree.text: Good. I remember that you were worth trusting. I will hold to that, even if I cannot hold to much else.

disagree.speaker: Varn
disagree.text: Then we are further from each other than we were before. I remember you being worth the trouble. Perhaps I misremembered.
